Name: information_theoretic_model_compression
Title: Exploring the Fundamental Limits of Model Compression: An Information-Theoretic Approach
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of model compression can be characterized using information-theoretic tools, such as rate-distortion theory and entropy, and that these limits can be achieved in practice using novel compression algorithms and techniques.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, AMP-ViT, and SNIP have explored various aspects of model compression and quantization, but have not fully addressed the fundamental limits of compression from an information-theoretic perspective. Our work builds upon these efforts and seeks to establish a theoretical foundation for model compression.
Abstract: This research proposal explores the fundamental limits of model compression using information-theoretic tools. We aim to characterize the rate-distortion tradeoff of model compression and develop novel compression algorithms and techniques that achieve these limits in practice. Our approach will involve a combination of theoretical analysis, algorithm design, and experimental evaluation. We will investigate the use of entropy, mutual information, and other information-theoretic measures to quantify the compressibility of models and develop methods to optimize compression ratios while preserving model accuracy.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments to evaluate the performance of our compression algorithms and techniques on a range of models and datasets. We will use metrics such as compression ratio, accuracy, and computational complexity to assess the effectiveness of our approach. We will also compare our results to state-of-the-art compression methods and provide a thorough analysis of the tradeoffs between compression ratio, accuracy, and computational complexity.
Risk Factors and Limitations: One potential risk factor is that our approach may not be able to achieve the theoretical limits of compression in practice due to the complexity of real-world models and datasets. Another limitation is that our approach may require significant computational resources and expertise in information theory and compression algorithms.
